children.heading: Transportation Provided at Rates Other Than Legal Tariff Rates.—
children.label: a Transportation Provided at Rates Other Than Legal Tariff Rates.—
A
children.text: the carrier or freight forwarder is no longer transporting property or is transporting property for the purpose of avoiding the application of this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: the person was offered a transportation rate by the carrier or freight forwarder other than that legally on file at the time with the Board or with the Interstate Commerce Commission, as required, for the transportation service;

children.label: ii
children.text: the person tendered freight to the carrier or freight forwarder in reasonable reliance upon the offered transportation rate;

children.label: iii
children.text: the carrier or freight forwarder did not properly or timely file with the Board or with the Interstate Commerce Commission, as required, a tariff providing for such transportation rate or failed to enter into an agreement for contract carriage;

children.label: iv
children.text: such transportation rate was billed and collected by the carrier or freight forwarder; and

children.label: v
children.text: the carrier or freight forwarder demands additional payment of a higher rate filed in a tariff.

children.heading: .—Forum
children.label: 2 .—Forum
children.text: If there is a dispute as to the showing under paragraph (1)(A), such dispute shall be resolved by the court in which the claim is brought. If there is a dispute as to the showing under paragraph (1)(B), such dispute shall be resolved by the Board. Pending the resolution of any such dispute, the person shall not have to pay any additional compensation to the carrier or freight forwarder.

children.heading: .—Effect of satisfaction of claims
children.label: 3 .—Effect of satisfaction of claims
children.text: Satisfaction of the claim under subsection (b), (c), or (d) shall be binding on the parties, and the parties shall not be subject to chapter 119 of this title, as such chapter was in effect on , or chapter 149.December 31, 1995

children.heading: 10,000 .—Claims Involving Shipments WeighingPounds or Less
children.label: b 10,000 .—Claims Involving Shipments WeighingPounds or Less
children.text: A person from whom the additional legally applicable and effective tariff rate or charges are sought may elect to satisfy the claim if the shipments each weighed 10,000 pounds or less, by payment of 20 percent of the difference between the carrier’s applicable and effective tariff rate and the rate originally billed and paid. In the event that a dispute arises as to the rate that was legally applicable to the shipment, such dispute shall be resolved by the Board.

children.heading: 10,000 .—Claims Involving Shipments Weighing More ThanPounds
children.label: c 10,000 .—Claims Involving Shipments Weighing More ThanPounds
children.text: A person from whom the additional legally applicable and effective tariff rate or charges are sought may elect to satisfy the claim if the shipments each weighed more than 10,000 pounds, by payment of 15 percent of the difference between the carrier’s applicable and effective tariff rate and the rate originally billed and paid. In the event that a dispute arises as to the rate that was legally applicable to the shipment, such dispute shall be resolved by the Board.

children.heading: .—Claims Involving Public Warehousemen
children.label: d .—Claims Involving Public Warehousemen
children.text: Notwithstanding subsections (b) and (c), a person from whom the additional legally applicable and effective tariff rate or charges are sought may elect to satisfy the claim by payment of 5 percent of the difference between the carrier’s applicable and effective tariff rate and the rate originally billed and paid if such person is a public warehouseman. In the event that a dispute arises as to the rate that was legally applicable to the shipment, such dispute shall be resolved by the Board.

children.heading: .—Effects of Election
children.label: e .—Effects of Election
children.text: When a person from whom additional legally applicable freight rates or charges are sought does not elect to use the provisions of subsection (b), (c) or (d), the person may pursue all rights and remedies existing under this part or, for transportation provided before , all rights and remedies that existed under this title on .January 1, 1996December 31, 1995

children.heading: .—Stay of Additional Compensation
children.label: f .—Stay of Additional Compensation
children.text: When a person proceeds under this section to challenge the reasonableness of the legally applicable freight rate or charges being claimed by a carrier or freight forwarder in addition to those already billed and collected, the person shall not have to pay any additional compensation to the carrier or freight forwarder until the Board has made a determination as to the reasonableness of the challenged rate as applied to the freight of the person against whom the claim is made.

children.heading: .—General rule
children.label: 1 .—General rule
children.text: A person must notify the carrier or freight forwarder as to its election to proceed under subsection (b), (c), or (d). Except as provided in paragraphs (2), (3), and (4), such election may be made at any time.

children.label: A
children.text: the 60th day following the filing of an answer to a suit for the collection of such additional legally applicable freight rate or charges, or

children.label: B
children.text: .March 5, 1994

children.heading: .—Pending suits for collection made beforedecember 4, 1993
children.label: 3 .—Pending suits for collection made beforedecember 4, 1993
children.text: If the carrier or freight forwarder or party representing such carrier or freight forwarder has filed, before , a suit for the collection of additional freight charges and notifies the person from whom additional freight charges are sought of the provisions of subsections (a) through (f), the election must be made not later than the 90th day following the date on which such notification is received.December 4, 1993

children.label: A
children.text: the 60th day following the filing of an answer to a suit for the collection of such additional legally applicable freight rate or charges, or

children.label: B
children.text: .March 5, 1994

children.heading: Claims Involving Small-Business Concerns, Charitable Organizations, and Re­cyclable Materials.—
children.label: h Claims Involving Small-Business Concerns, Charitable Organizations, and Re­cyclable Materials.—
A
children.text: if such person qualifies as a small-business concern under the Small Business Act ( et seq.),15 U.S.C. 631

children.label: B
children.text: if such person is an organization which is described in section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and exempt from tax under section 501(a) of such Code, or

children.label: C
children.text: if the cargo involved in the claim is recyclable materials.

children.heading: .—Recyclable materials defined
children.label: 2 .—Recyclable materials defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “recyclable materials” means waste products for recycling or reuse in the furtherance of recognized pollution control programs.
